steps.title: Identify the three-part structure
steps.content: The text first defines what community science is and calls it effective, then lists concrete benefits (conservation assistance, youth learning, increased data collection), and finally provides a specific example: the Grace Herzel butterfly study in Arkansas that used this approach.

steps.title: Match the structure to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D identifies a particular approach to research, lists some benefits of that approach, and then mentions a study in which that approach was used — this matches the text exactly.

Choices A, B, and C either introduce content not present (A adds public opinion commentary; C adds development narrative and future suggestions) or misframe the primary focus (B treats a study as the main subject rather than as an example). D is correct.
